title: RechargeRecovery

buildability.reason: The app leverages existing Recharge webhook events, Shopify API for order data, and Postscript/Klaviyo for SMS/email, requiring no custom UI or heavy backend, making a 3‑person team able to deliver a functional v1 within 3 days, 3 weeks, and 10 weeks.

speedToRevenue.reason: It can be listed in the Shopify App Store as a self‑serve app, and merchants pay a subscription fee immediately after installation, enabling revenue in the first billing cycle.

wedge.reason: It intercepts Recharge failed‑charge webhooks and runs a smart multi‑channel recovery sequence, instantly reducing churn for merchants.

distribution.reason: Recharge already serves a large DTC merchant base; the app can be promoted via the App Store and content marketing to reach $50K–$2M merchants.

trapRisk.reason: The solution avoids the listed traps (no generative 3D/AR, no real‑time voice/video, no autonomous repricing, no three‑app scope).

expansion.reason: The architecture supports adding new recovery channels, payment methods, and analytics, enabling growth from €10K to €1M without pivoting.

technicalSnapshot: The app is built on Shopify’s API and App Store extensions, listens to Recharge’s failed‑charge webhook, uses the Visa/MC Account Updater API for payment updates, and integrates with Postscript and Klaviyo for SMS and email, all orchestrated via a lightweight Node.js backend.

v1Plan.day3: Create Shopify app skeleton, register webhook for Recharge failed‑charge, implement basic SMS via Postscript and email via Klaviyo.
v1Plan.week3: Add Visa/MC Account Updater API integration, implement smart retry logic, and build pause‑vs‑cancel decision based on LTV tier.
v1Plan.week10: Deploy production‑ready multi‑channel recovery workflow, add admin UI for configuration, provide analytics dashboard, and support multiple merchants.
